expectations.text: ownership-gap-named: Framed as structural ownership gap, not anyone's fault
expectations.evidence: 'exists in the space between teams, with no team or individual having formally claimed responsibility' and 'None of us have a shared rate limiting strategy' — structural framing throughout.
'Three teams (including mine) are shipping features in the next three weeks' and 'first feature shipping in roughly three weeks, we're past the point where "let's align soon" is enough.'
'I've raised this in #architecture-discuss twice over the past few weeks and got good engagement each time, but no one has stepped up to own the strategy.'

expectations.text: asks-for-owner-not-solution: Asks for ownership assignment, not technical solution
expectations.evidence: 'either a named owner who will drive a lightweight shared approach in the next week, or a call with the three teams to agree on one together' — ownership and coordination ask, not a technical fix request.

expectations.text: bluf-first-sentence: The first sentence states the situation and impact without preamble
expectations.evidence: 'I want to flag a cross-team risk that needs an owner before it becomes a production problem.' — opens with risk and consequence immediately.
